title: Syracuse partners with exalt to support at-risk youth: What we know now
summary: Syracuse Common Council approved $1.5M in state funding plus cannabis reinvestment dollars to launch exalt, a criminal justice diversion program for court-involved youth ages 15-19. The NYC-based non-profit will provide education support, paid internships ($18-20/hour), and workforce development to help at-risk youth avoid incarceration. Initial rollout targets 10 students with scaling planned as local staffing expands.

entity_refs.name: New York State Division of Criminal Justice Services

political_signal.description: Council leadership (Majok) expresses commitment to accountability and measurable outcomes. Mayoral transition alignment indicated by Mayor-Elect Owens' endorsement. Strong intergovernmental funding coordination ($1.5M state + cannabis reinvestment).
